brief_justification: Wrong answer (desk instead of drawer) but reasoning about Lisa's movement is sound; Goes one level beyond surface by tracking object movements and Matthew's perspective; No self-monitoring or questioning of assumptions; No verification against alternative possibilities; Presents conclusion as certain with no uncertainty acknowledgment; Does not consider the drawer as an alternative or evaluate what Matthew actually observed vs missed; Shows the reasoning chain (Lisa moved pen, Matthew saw it) but fails to consider Matthew's knowledge state properly — he was watching a movie with headphones when Lisa moved the pen, so he likely didn't observe the move and would default to the drawer as the pen's known location.
